events.text: Q1 FY2026 print: room nights 338M (+6% y/y, ~+8% ex-Middle East conflict, which cost ~2pts); gross bookings $53.8B (+15%); revenue $5.5B (+16%); adj. EBITDA ~$1.3B (+19%, above the top of the guide); adj. EPS $1.14 (+14%, post 25-for-1 split). The US accelerated for the 4th straight quarter to low-teens.

Fogel argues that AI is a net positive, not a threat: incumbent scale plus proprietary intent data improve conversion, and travel discovery via LLMs expands the digital TAM ("35%... 45%... higher"). Responding to Justin Post (BofA), he stresses that frontier players are moving toward performance marketing/advertising, the Google-style auction model in which BKNG thrives.

Connected Trip +high-teens (~3x total transaction growth); air tickets +28%, attractions ~+25%. Penny (Priceline) is already booking accommodation/flights directly with an early conversion lift (small sample). Agoda cut its servicing cost per booking by double digits via AI automation.

Q2'26 guide: room nights +2-4% (conflict ~3pts headwind; underlying ~5-7%), GBV/revenue/EBITDA each +4-6%. FY26 lowered at the midpoint but the high end of GBV and adj. EPS held. Steenbergen: the assumption of impact through end-June with recovery in 2H is explicitly uncertain ("anyone can put whatever you want in your model").
Record $3.6B buyback in Q1 (+$343M dividend); share count down >40% since 2014 at an average price of ~$93. Cash/investments $16.5B.
Justin Post (BofA) flagged that large AI players are "moving away from transactions," raising the structural risk of agentic disintermediation; BKNG's proof points are still from a very small sample.

cells: Booking Assistant in ChatGPT App Store
Penny books direct, early lift; Claude ad placement
